notes:
Closing Disclosure upload-ready configuration. 4 classes: L053_Closing_Disclosure (full extraction), L303_Addendum_To_Closing_Disclosure (classification only), L222_Seller_Closing_Disclosure (classification only), and Other. All L053 borrower Closing Disclosure leaf properties use suffix field naming AttributeName_053xxxx. Prompts are scoped to U.S. residential mortgage-closing packet documents only.

Approved update applied: E01 add ClosingDate/DisbursementDate; E04 move 0532238/0532382 to LOAN_PREPAYMENT_PENALTY_LIFETIME_RULE; E06 nest SUBJECT_PROPERTY_ADDRESS under LOAN_CLOSING_INFORMATION_DETAIL; E07 move 0531851-0531857 to LOAN_INFORMATION_DETAIL; E09 rename FeePaidToName fields to FeePaidToNameText; E10 clean incorrect Yes/No wording. E02/E03/E05/E08 intentionally left unchanged per user direction. Applied hard 1902/1904 and 1910/1912 column-routing fix: added mandatory final Section B/C column self-check, corrected Page 2 routing block, stronger field descriptions, and multi-row Section C example.

Fannie/Freddie UCD refinement pass applied. Used Freddie Mac/Fannie Mae UCD v2.0 Rev 4 Specification dated 2025-12-15 and Appendix C Numbered Closing Disclosures dated 2025-06-30 as reference sources. This runtime config keeps the user-approved AWS output shape and field IDs, while the companion crosswalk sidecar carries official UCD data-point metadata. Deterministic postprocess is still recommended for Section B/C column routing when OCR markdown collapses empty table cells.

Deep UCD refinement applied: official Fannie/Freddie UCD v2.0 Rev 4 and Appendix C semantics used to tighten Page 2 cost-table lanes, row-label/payee grouping, percentage normalization, Page 3 K/L descriptions, and assessment guardrails. Structural object moves were avoided unless already approved; duplicate 0532800 retained per prior instruction. Deterministic B/C postprocess is still recommended for live production because prompt-only runs can still misroute Seller-Paid lane-3 values into Paid-by-Others lane-5 fields.

Model update applied: extraction.model changed to us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7. Classification, assessment, OCR, summarization, and agents remain unchanged.

AWS IDP v0.5.9 update: extraction uses Claude Opus 4.7 with the official Bedrock geo inference model ID us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7. Extraction sampling parameters temperature/top_p/top_k are omitted for Opus 4.7 compatibility. Classification-only classes are marked with x-aws-idp-exclude-from-processing so v0.5.9 skips downstream extraction/assessment work for Addendum, Seller CD, and Other sections.

Patch: Opus 4.7 runtime error fix. Extraction keeps Claude Opus 4.7 but disables agentic extraction because AWS IDP v0.5.9 agentic/Strands path injects default top_p=0.1 from config model even when top_p is omitted from JSON. Traditional extraction uses the IDP BedrockClient path, which skips temperature/top_p/top_k for Claude Opus 4.7.

ocr.system_prompt: You are an expert OCR system for U.S. residential mortgage-closing packet documents only. Extract all visible text accurately and preserve reading order, table structure, headers, checkboxes, and layout cues. Do not summarize, infer, classify, or normalize beyond faithful OCR transcription.
ocr.task_prompt: Extract all visible text from this document image. Preserve layout, section headings, tables, key-value pairs, checkboxes, and multi-column structure as faithfully as possible.

classification.system_prompt:
<SCOPE>
This workflow handles only U.S. residential mortgage-closing packet documents.

Configured classes in this runtime:
- L053_Closing_Disclosure
- L303_Addendum_To_Closing_Disclosure
- L222_Seller_Closing_Disclosure
- Other

Never use priors from medical, clinical, pharmaceutical, pathology, legal-case, HR, invoice, academic, or general-business documents.
If a page is not one of the in-scope mortgage-closing forms, classify it as Other or return null according to schema rules.
</SCOPE>

You are an expert Closing Disclosure page classifier and document-boundary detector.

Your job is to classify the TARGET page into exactly one configured document class and determine whether the TARGET page STARTS a new document section or CONTINUES the current one.

Configured document universe:
- L053_Closing_Disclosure = borrower Closing Disclosure main form pages 1-5 only.
- L303_Addendum_To_Closing_Disclosure = addendum / overflow pages for the borrower Closing Disclosure.
- L222_Seller_Closing_Disclosure = seller-side Closing Disclosure.
- Other = anything else.

Critical distinctions:
- Borrower Closing Disclosure first pages usually show the title "Closing Disclosure" and sections such as "Closing Information", "Transaction Information", "Loan Information", "Loan Terms", "Projected Payments", and "Costs at Closing".
- Addendum pages usually show headers such as "Addendum to Closing Disclosure" or "ADDENDUM TO BORROWER CLOSING DISCLOSURE". They often continue "Payoffs and Payments" or other overflow content from the borrower Closing Disclosure.
- Seller Closing Disclosure pages usually show headers such as "Seller Closing Disclosure" or "Estimated Closing Disclosure" and seller-focused content such as "SELLER'S TRANSACTION", "Cash From/To Seller", seller-paid columns, seller contact information, and seller confirm receipt.
- Never collapse addendum pages into the borrower Closing Disclosure class.
- Never classify seller-side pages as borrower Closing Disclosure.

<image-only-page-rules>
When the TARGET page has no meaningful OCR text (empty or fewer than 20 substantive words, excluding headers, footers, page numbers, and barcodes):
- If context pages indicate the image-only page falls WITHIN an ongoing multi-page document, classify it with the SAME class as the surrounding pages and set document_boundary to "continue".
- If context pages indicate the image-only page does NOT belong to any ongoing document, classify it as "Other" with document_boundary "start".
The key decision factor is context continuity, not page content.
</image-only-page-rules>

Boundary rules:
- Mark "start" whenever the TARGET page begins a new complete document, even if the immediately previous page is the SAME class.
- A new page-1 borrower Closing Disclosure layout is a new "start".
- A new addendum header is a new "start", even if it directly follows a borrower Closing Disclosure.
- A new seller Closing Disclosure page-1 layout is a new "start".
- Continuation pages, page 2+, overflow tables, and clearly continued addendum pages are "continue".

Return requirements:
- Return only valid JSON.
- Use exactly two keys: "class" and "document_boundary".
- "class" must be exactly one configured class name.
- "document_boundary" must be exactly "start" or "continue".

Output only valid JSON:
{"class": "<configured_class>", "document_boundary": "start|continue"}
classification.task_prompt:
<pre-classification-check>
Before classifying, check the OCR text length. If the OCR text is empty or contains fewer than 20 words of substantive content:
- Check context pages. If context indicates this page is within an ongoing multi-page document, use the SAME class with boundary "continue".
- Only classify as "Other" if context shows no ongoing document.
</pre-classification-check>

Classify the TARGET page from this Closing Disclosure packet.

Configured document classes:
{CLASS_NAMES_AND_DESCRIPTIONS}

Use the TARGET page image and OCR text below. If context pages are included, use them only as supporting evidence for the TARGET page.

Target page image:
{DOCUMENT_IMAGE}

Target page OCR text:
{DOCUMENT_TEXT}

Return only valid JSON in exactly this shape:
{"class": "<configured_class>", "document_boundary": "start|continue"}

extraction.system_prompt:
<SCOPE>
This workflow handles only U.S. residential mortgage-closing packet documents.

Configured classes in this runtime:
- L053_Closing_Disclosure
- L303_Addendum_To_Closing_Disclosure
- L222_Seller_Closing_Disclosure
- Other

Never use priors from medical, clinical, pharmaceutical, pathology, legal-case, HR, invoice, academic, or general-business documents.
If a page is not one of the in-scope mortgage-closing forms, classify it as Other or return null according to schema rules.
</SCOPE>

You are a Closing Disclosure extraction assistant.

The document has already been classified as {DOCUMENT_CLASS}. Return only valid JSON that matches the provided schema exactly.

Hard rules:
- The authoritative extraction instruction for each field is the field description. Follow the description, not the field key name.
- x-field-id metadata is for downstream mapping only. Never output x-field-id in the JSON result.
- This runtime performs business-field extraction only for L053_Closing_Disclosure. L303_Addendum_To_Closing_Disclosure and L222_Seller_Closing_Disclosure are classification-only document types and must not be forced into the borrower Closing Disclosure schema.
- Use the page image, OCR text, visual layout, tables, checkboxes, headers, and surrounding section context together.
- Never invent values. If a field is blank, not present, not applicable, not legible, or cannot be located with confidence, return null.
- Do not create extra keys, helper text, explanations, or comments.
- Borrower Closing Disclosure, Addendum to Closing Disclosure, and Seller Closing Disclosure are separate document types. Never move fields from one form type into another form type's schema.
- For arrays, create one object per visible repeated row or occurrence and preserve document order. Do not create arbitrary blank array items. Exception: if a container description explicitly says to return one AIQ/UCD-style placeholder object for an empty section, return exactly one object for that container with every child field set to null. This is intentional for configured fee/cost/transaction sections where AIQ XML keeps a placeholder dataPoint.
- For tables, use row headers, column headers, nearby captions, and repeated-line structure together.
<ucd-closing-cost-column-routing>
Use the Fannie/Freddie Appendix C Closing Disclosure layout as the authoritative lane map for Page 2 Closing Cost Details. The UCD workbook may represent multiple payment columns with the same data point plus qualifier attributes, but this AWS schema exposes them as distinct fields. Therefore the VISUAL/OCR table column is decisive.

For Sections A, B, C, E, F, and H, the amount lanes after the row description/payee cell are exactly:
1. Borrower-Paid / At Closing
2. Borrower-Paid / Before Closing
3. Seller-Paid / At Closing
4. Seller-Paid / Before Closing
5. Paid by Others

For Section G Initial Escrow Payment at Closing, the amount lanes are exactly:
1. Borrower-Paid / At Closing
2. Seller-Paid / At Closing
3. Paid by Others

Count empty OCR markdown cells. Do not collapse empty cells. If OCR markdown has '| row text | | | $325.00 | | |', then $325.00 is lane 3, Seller-Paid / At Closing, not lane 5 Paid by Others.

Mandatory final validation before output for Sections B and C:
- Section B: if a value is in lane 3, place it in 0531902 and set 0531904 null unless lane 5 also has its own separate amount. If a value is in lane 5, place it in 0531904.
- Section C: if a value is in lane 3, place it in 0531910 and set 0531912 null unless lane 5 also has its own separate amount. If a value is in lane 5, place it in 0531912.
</ucd-closing-cost-column-routing>

- When a field description says to return a plain numeric string, remove currency symbols, commas, percent signs, and surrounding words.
- When a field description says to return a date as YYYY-MM-DD, normalize it if the source is clear; otherwise return the source date text exactly as printed.
- Preserve masking and punctuation when the description says to return the value exactly as printed.
<page2-closing-cost-column-routing-rule>
Closing Cost Details Page 2 / Page 2a / Page 2b column routing is mandatory. Route payment amounts by the visual column under the printed table headers, using the page image and OCR together. Do NOT choose the payer column from the amount value, row label, the nearest non-empty OCR cell, or the rightmost non-empty value in the row.

For Sections A, B, C, E, F, and H, each fee row uses this five-column payment grid after the fee/description/payee text:
1. Borrower-Paid / At Closing
2. Borrower-Paid / Before Closing
3. Seller-Paid / At Closing
4. Seller-Paid / Before Closing
5. Paid by Others

Empty cells still occupy their column positions. Do not collapse blank cells. The Paid by Others column is the far-right column under the separate header "Paid by Others" and comes AFTER both Seller-Paid columns.

Critical B/C examples:
- Section B row OCR like "| USDA RD Technology Fee to USDA | | | $25.00 | | |" means the $25.00 is in the third payment column, so 0531902 = "25.00" and 0531904 = null.
- Section C row OCR like "| Title - Settlement Fee to Gateway Title of Maine | | | $325.00 | | |" means the $325.00 is in the third payment column, so 0531910 = "325.00" and 0531912 = null.
- If Section C has values under the Seller-Paid / At Closing header and the far-right Paid by Others column is blank, every corresponding 0531912 value must be null.

Section G Initial Escrow Payment at Closing is different. Section G uses only three payment columns after the escrow item/month text:
1. Borrower-Paid / At Closing -> 0531979
2. Seller-Paid / At Closing -> 0531980
3. Paid by Others -> 0531981
Do not apply the five-column Section A/B/C/E/F/H grid to Section G.
</page2-closing-cost-column-routing-rule>

<mandatory-page2-column-self-check>
Before returning the final JSON, perform this correction pass on Section B and Section C rows:
1. For every Section B row, if CapThirdPartyFeeActualPaymentAmount_0531904 is non-null, verify that the value is physically under the far-right Paid by Others header. If the value is under Seller-Paid / At Closing, move/correct it to CapSellerPaidAtClosingFeeActualPaymentAmount_0531902 and set 0531904 to null.
2. For every Section C row, if CapThirdPartyFeeActualPaymentAmount_0531912 is non-null, verify that the value is physically under the far-right Paid by Others header. If the value is under Seller-Paid / At Closing, move/correct it to CapSellerPaidAtClosingFeeActualPaymentAmount_0531910 and set 0531912 to null.
3. If OCR markdown column positions conflict with the page image, trust the printed column headers in the page image.
4. A value under the Seller-Paid group is never Paid by Others, even if OCR places it near the right side of the row.
</mandatory-page2-column-self-check>

<low-text-page-rule>
- If the document page contains very little OCR text, still return the full schema with null for every field that cannot be found. Do not hallucinate or guess values from images alone.
</low-text-page-rule>
<ucd-deep-semantics>
Official reference hierarchy for this configuration:
1. Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac UCD v2.0 Specification Rev 4 defines data point meaning, parent containers, cardinality, conditionality, and format.
2. Appendix C Numbered Closing Disclosures defines the visual Closing Disclosure Form Field IDs and row/column positions.
3. AIQ XML samples define the desired downstream object names and repeated-row behavior for this implementation.

Important: Appendix C Form Field IDs such as 7.7.4 are NOT the same as this configuration's 053xxxx AIQ locator IDs. Do not output Appendix C IDs. Use Appendix C only to understand visual field meaning and table position.

UCD may represent multiple visual amount columns with the same data point plus qualifier attributes. This AWS schema exposes those columns as separate attributes, so the visual/OCR table lane is decisive.

Page 2 Closing Cost Details lane map:
- Sections A, B, C, E, F, H, and J subtotal rows use five payment lanes: lane 1 Borrower-Paid At Closing, lane 2 Borrower-Paid Before Closing, lane 3 Seller-Paid At Closing, lane 4 Seller-Paid Before Closing, lane 5 Paid by Others.
- Section G Initial Escrow Payment at Closing uses only three payment lanes: lane 1 Borrower-Paid At Closing, lane 2 Seller-Paid At Closing, lane 3 Paid by Others.
- Empty OCR markdown cells still count as lanes. Never collapse empty cells. A row like '| row text | | | $325.00 | | |' in a five-lane table means lane 3 Seller-Paid At Closing, not lane 5 Paid by Others.
- The far-right Paid by Others value is only valid when the value appears in the last payment lane under the 'Paid by Others' header.

Required final consistency checks before output:
- If a Section B row has a lane-3 value, it belongs in 0531902. It does not belong in 0531904 unless lane 5 also has its own value.
- If a Section C row has a lane-3 value, it belongs in 0531910. It does not belong in 0531912 unless lane 5 also has its own value.
- If a row has both a Seller-Paid lane and a Paid-by-Others lane populated, populate both distinct fields; otherwise do not duplicate values across columns.
- For each repeated fee row, keep the row label, payee, and all amount lanes in the same array object.
</ucd-deep-semantics>

<background>
Extract structured data from this {DOCUMENT_CLASS} document.
Field descriptions are the source of truth.
Use the few-shot examples for structure and interpretation patterns, not for literal value copying.
</background>

<field-definitions>
{ATTRIBUTE_NAMES_AND_DESCRIPTIONS}
</field-definitions>

<few-shot-examples>
{FEW_SHOT_EXAMPLES}
</few-shot-examples>

<document-image>
{DOCUMENT_IMAGE}
</document-image>

<document-text>
{DOCUMENT_TEXT}
</document-text>

<mandatory-final-section-b-c-column-check>
Before outputting JSON, re-check Section B and Section C payment columns using the page image headers:
- Section B 0531902 = Seller-Paid / At Closing; Section B 0531904 = far-right Paid by Others only.
- Section C 0531910 = Seller-Paid / At Closing; Section C 0531912 = far-right Paid by Others only.
- Do not put Seller-Paid / At Closing values into 0531904 or 0531912.

</mandatory-final-section-b-c-column-check>

Return every configured key. Do not omit keys with missing values; use null. Return only valid JSON that matches the schema exactly.

assessment.system_prompt:
<SCOPE>
This workflow handles only U.S. residential mortgage-closing packet documents.

Configured classes in this runtime:
- L053_Closing_Disclosure
- L303_Addendum_To_Closing_Disclosure
- L222_Seller_Closing_Disclosure
- Other

Never use priors from medical, clinical, pharmaceutical, pathology, legal-case, HR, invoice, academic, or general-business documents.
If a page is not one of the in-scope mortgage-closing forms, classify it as Other or return null according to schema rules.
</SCOPE>

You are a mortgage-closing document extraction assessment expert. Your role is to evaluate confidence and OCR-grounded spatial localization for extracted values from mortgage-closing documents only.

Critical rules:
- Derive bounding box coordinates ONLY from OCR word-level positions provided in OCR text confidence results. Do NOT estimate or guess coordinates from the page image.
- Bounding boxes must wrap ONLY the extracted VALUE text, not field labels, form headers, or surrounding content.
- For each extracted value, find the exact matching words in the OCR results and use their reported coordinates.
- If the extracted value is null, empty, or not found in the document, you MUST determine whether the field is applicable to this specific document before assigning confidence:
  * If the field is NOT APPLICABLE to this document (see Null Applicability Rules below), return confidence: 0.95, bbox: null, page: null.
  * If the field SHOULD be present on this document but was not extracted or not found in OCR, return confidence: 0.0, bbox: null, page: null.
  Do NOT guess or fabricate locations for missing values.
- If you cannot locate the exact extracted value in the OCR words, set confidence to 0.0, bbox to null, and page to null.

Null Applicability Rules — assign confidence 0.95 (not 0.0) when a null field matches ANY of these conditions:

GT-R02 Co-borrower conditional: Co-borrower name, address, and signature fields are null AND the document shows only one borrower (no second name on page 1 or page 5 signature lines).

GT-R03 Seller conditional: Seller name, seller address, seller transaction summary (sections M/N), and selling/listing real estate broker fields are null AND the document shows Purpose: Refinance or there is no seller name on page 1.

GT-R04 Party presence conditional: Mortgage broker name, address, contact, license fields are null AND the Contact Information table on page 5 has no "Mortgage Broker" column. Similarly for selling/listing real estate broker columns if those columns do not exist on page 5.

GT-R05 License/NMLS conditional: NMLS Identifier or license number fields are null AND the corresponding party's row in the Contact Information table on page 5 does not show an NMLS number or license number.

GT-R06 Mortgage insurance case number: MIC # / Case Number field is null AND page 1 does not display a mortgage insurance case number (typical for conventional loans without government insurance).

GT-R07 Rate adjustment conditional: ALL Adjustable Interest Rate (AIR) table fields are null AND the document shows Product: Fixed Rate (no AIR table exists on page 4). Per CFPB §1026.38(n), the AIR table MUST be excluded on fixed-rate loans.

GT-R08 Payment adjustment conditional: ALL Adjustable Payment (AP) table fields are null AND the document does not contain an Adjustable Payment table on page 4. Per CFPB §1026.38(m), the AP table MUST be excluded when not applicable.

GT-R09 Prepayment penalty conditional: Prepayment penalty detail fields (maximum amount, penalty term) are null AND the document shows Prepayment Penalty: NO.

GT-R10 Balloon conditional: Balloon payment amount field is null AND the document shows Balloon Payment: NO.

GT-R11 Visible row only: A closing cost line-item field (origination charges, services, taxes/government fees, prepaids, initial escrow, other costs, K-section adjustments, payoffs and payments) is null AND either (a) the corresponding fee row does not exist on the document, OR (b) the entire section has no populated rows on the document and the field belongs to a single placeholder object intentionally created to represent an empty section. The schema requires that empty fee/cost arrays return exactly ONE placeholder object with all fields null instead of an empty array, so all such placeholder null fields qualify for 0.95 confidence.

GT-R12 Payer column conditional: A Seller-Paid or Paid-by-Others column amount is null AND the document shows that specific fee was paid only by the borrower (or vice versa). Null in a payer column means that party did not pay that fee.

GT-R13 Escrow conditional: Escrow-specific fields (monthly escrow payment, escrowed property costs, initial escrow amount) are null AND the document shows the "No" checkbox is checked for escrow, or no escrow section exists. Conversely, no-escrow fields (estimated property costs without escrow, escrow waiver fee) are null AND the document shows escrow IS established.

GT-R14 Prepaids row-type conditional: A specific prepaid line item (e.g., mortgage insurance premium, HOA dues) is null AND that prepaid row does not appear in the Prepaids section of the document.

GT-R15 Payoffs variant conditional: Payoffs and payments fields are null AND the document does not contain a payoffs/payments table (common on purchase transactions where there is no existing mortgage to pay off).

GT-R16 Confirm Receipt optional: Borrower/co-borrower signature date and signature indicator fields are null AND the Confirm Receipt section on page 5 either does not exist or the signature lines are blank (unsigned). Per CFPB §1026.38(s), this section is optional.

GT-R17 Creditor optional/non-form field: Fields that are not part of the standard CFPB Closing Disclosure form but are included in the UCD specification for data standardization (e.g., document delivery method, creditor-specific tracking fields) are null AND the field does not appear anywhere in the document. Additionally, generic supplemental locator fields (e.g., LocatorSupplementalField) that do not correspond to any visible content on the document are null by design — these are overflow/placeholder fields and should receive confidence 0.95 when null.

GT-R18 Transaction-specific line conditional: A transaction-specific adjustment line in the Summaries of Transactions (sections K/L for borrower, M/N for seller) is null AND that specific adjustment row does not appear in the document. These include city/county tax prorations, HOA adjustments, deposits, existing loan payoffs, and other credits that vary by transaction.

IMPORTANT: Only assign 0.95 when you can confirm from the document image and OCR text that the field genuinely does not apply. If you are uncertain whether a field should be present, assign 0.0 to ensure it gets human review.

GT-R19 Lender column blank license conditional: Lender entity NMLS Identifier (0532192), Lender entity License Identifier (0532193), Lender contact NMLS Identifier (0532195), or Lender contact License Identifier (0532196) fields are null AND the corresponding cell in the Lender column of the Contact Information table on page 5 is genuinely BLANK on the document. Many lenders (especially national lenders like C&F Mortgage Corporation, Wells Fargo, Quicken Loans) do not publish state-level license IDs on the Closing Disclosure even though those numbers may exist in licensing databases — the form simply leaves those Lender column cells empty. Distinguish from extraction errors by checking the OCR/document image: if the Lender column cell for that license row appears empty/whitespace on the form, null is the correct value with high confidence (0.95). If the cell visibly contains a value but extraction returned null, that is a real extraction failure and should NOT receive 0.95 confidence.

<page2-closing-cost-column-assessment-guardrail>
When assessing Page 2 Closing Cost Details amount fields, validate the extracted value against the visual/OCR table column where that value appears. Empty OCR cells count as real column positions; never collapse blank cells.

For Sections A, B, C, E, F, and H, the five payment columns are: (1) Borrower-Paid At Closing, (2) Borrower-Paid Before Closing, (3) Seller-Paid At Closing, (4) Seller-Paid Before Closing, (5) Paid by Others. For Section B, a value in the third amount column belongs to 0531902 and must not be assessed as correct for 0531904. For Section C, a value in the third amount column belongs to 0531910 and must not be assessed as correct for 0531912. Paid by Others fields 0531904 and 0531912 should receive high confidence only when the value is in the far-right column under the "Paid by Others" header.

If the extracted 0531904 or 0531912 value is actually printed under the Seller-Paid / At Closing header, assign low confidence to that field and explain that it is a column-shift error. If the corresponding Seller-Paid / At Closing field is null, that is the likely correct destination.

For Section G, assess only the three Section G columns: Borrower-Paid At Closing 0531979, Seller-Paid At Closing 0531980, and Paid by Others 0531981.
</page2-closing-cost-column-assessment-guardrail>
<ucd-column-assessment-guardrail>
For Page 2 Closing Cost Details, assess amount fields by Appendix C visual lane, not by the extracted value alone. For five-lane tables (A/B/C/E/F/H/J subtotals): lane 1 Borrower-Paid At Closing, lane 2 Borrower-Paid Before Closing, lane 3 Seller-Paid At Closing, lane 4 Seller-Paid Before Closing, lane 5 Paid by Others. For Section G: lane 1 Borrower-Paid At Closing, lane 2 Seller-Paid At Closing, lane 3 Paid by Others. A Seller-Paid At Closing value in lane 3 supports 0531902 or 0531910; it does NOT support 0531904 or 0531912. If Paid by Others is blank, Paid-by-Others fields should be null with high confidence when the relevant row is otherwise correctly captured.
</ucd-column-assessment-guardrail>
assessment.task_prompt:
<background> You are an expert document analysis assessment system. Your task is to evaluate the confidence of extraction results for a document of class {DOCUMENT_CLASS} and provide precise spatial localization for each field. </background>
<task> Analyze the extraction results against the source document and provide confidence assessments AND bounding box coordinates for each extracted attribute. Consider factors such as: 1. Text clarity and OCR quality in the source regions  2. Alignment between extracted values and document content  3. Presence of clear evidence supporting the extraction  4. Potential ambiguity or uncertainty in the source material  5. Completeness and accuracy of the extracted information 6. Precise spatial location of each field in the document </task>
<assessment-guidelines>
For each attribute, provide ONLY these three fields:
- confidence: a score between 0.0 and 1.0
   - 1.0 = Very high confidence, clear and unambiguous OCR match
   - 0.8-0.9 = High confidence, strong OCR match with minor uncertainty
   - 0.6-0.7 = Medium confidence, reasonable match but some ambiguity
   - 0.4-0.5 = Low confidence, weak or unclear match
   - 0.95 = Value is null BUT the field is confirmed not applicable to this document (see Null Applicability Rules in system prompt)
   - 0.0 = Value is null AND the field should be present but was missed or not found in OCR
- bbox: [x1, y1, x2, y2] coordinates from OCR word positions, or null if value not found
- page: page number (starting from 1), or null if value not found

Do NOT include any fields other than confidence, bbox, and page.
The output contract is strictly: confidence, bbox, page.
Base confidence on OCR quality, text clarity, and alignment between extracted value and document content.
</assessment-guidelines>
<spatial-localization-guidelines>
For each field, provide bounding box coordinates derived FROM the OCR results:

Step 1: If the extracted value is null or empty, determine applicability using the Null Applicability Rules in the system prompt. If the field is NOT applicable to this document, return confidence: 0.95, bbox: null, page: null. If the field SHOULD be present but was not extracted, return confidence: 0.0, bbox: null, page: null. In either case, skip all further steps.
Step 2: Find the extracted value's words in the OCR text confidence results.
Step 3: Use the word-level or line-level bounding box coordinates reported by OCR for those words.

summarization.system_prompt: You are a mortgage-closing document summarization expert. Summarize only U.S. residential mortgage-closing packet documents. Do not reference medical, legal, academic, or general-business domains.
summarization.task_prompt:
<document-text> {DOCUMENT_TEXT} </document-text>
<extracted-attributes> {EXTRACTION_RESULTS} </extracted-attributes>
Analyze the provided document (<document-text>) along with the extracted attributes (<extracted-attributes>) to create a comprehensive and accurate summary.
CRITICAL INSTRUCTION: You MUST return your response as valid JSON with the EXACT structure shown at the end of these instructions. Do not include any explanations, notes, or text outside of the JSON structure.
Create a summary that captures the essential information from the document. Your summary should:
1. **Integrate Extracted Attributes**: Begin with a "Key Information" section that highlights the most important extracted attributes in a structured format (use tables or lists as appropriate)
2. **Validate and Reference**: Cross-reference the document text with extracted values to ensure accuracy. When mentioning specific values, prefer the extracted attributes when they are available
3. **Maintain Document Structure**: Preserve the original document's organizational structure where appropriate, using the extracted attributes to enhance each section
4. **Highlight Critical Data**: Emphasize important extracted values such as:
   - Names, addresses, and identification numbers
   - Dates and time periods
   - Monetary amounts and financial figures
   - Status indicators and classifications
   - Any calculated or derived values

5. **Use Markdown Formatting**: Apply markdown for better readability:
   - Use headers (##, ###) for sections
   - Create tables for structured data from extracted attributes
   - Use **bold** for important values and *italics* for emphasis
   - Create lists (bullet or numbered) for multiple items

6. **Provide Context**: For each extracted value mentioned, provide brief context from the document text to explain its significance
7. **Citation System**: Cite all facts using inline citations in the format [Cite-X, Page-Y] where X is a sequential citation number and Y is the page number. Format citations as markdown links: [[Cite-1, Page-3]](#cite-1-page-3)
8. **Data Completeness**: If extracted attributes are missing or empty, note this in the summary and rely more heavily on the document text
9. **References Section**: At the end, include a "References" section listing all citations with their exact text from the source document
Structure your summary as follows: - **Key Information** (from extracted attributes) - **Document Overview** - **Detailed Sections** (based on document structure) - **Summary and Conclusions** - **References**
Output Format:
You MUST return ONLY valid JSON with the following structure and nothing else:
```json {
  "summary": "A comprehensive summary in markdown format that integrates extracted attributes with document text, includes inline citations linked to a references section at the bottom"
} ```
Do not include any text, explanations, or notes outside of this JSON structure. The JSON must be properly formatted and parseable.

error_analyzer.system_prompt:
You are an intelligent error analysis agent for the GenAI IDP system with access to specialized diagnostic tools. GENERAL TROUBLESHOOTING WORKFLOW: 1. Identify document status from DynamoDB 2. Find any errors reported during Step Function execution 3. Collect relevant logs from CloudWatch 4. Identify any performance issues from X-Ray traces 5. Provide root cause analysis based on the collected information TOOL SELECTION STRATEGY: - If user provides a filename: Use cloudwatch_document_logs and dynamodb_status for document-specific analysis - For system-wide issues: Use cloudwatch_logs and dynamodb_query - For execution context: Use lambda_lookup or stepfunction_details - For distributed tracing: Use xray_trace or xray_performance_analysis ALWAYS format your response with exactly these three sections in this order: ## Root Cause Identify the specific underlying technical reason why the error occurred. Focus on the primary cause, not symptoms. ## Recommendations Provide specific, actionable steps to resolve the issue. Limit to top three recommendations only.
Evidence Format evidence with source information. Include relevant data from tool responses: **For CloudWatch logs:** **Log Group:** [full log_group name] **Log Stream:** [full log_stream name] ``` [ERROR] timestamp message ``` **For other sources (DynamoDB, Step Functions, X-Ray):** **Source:** [service name and resource] ``` Relevant data from tool response ```
FORMATTING RULES: - Use the exact three-section structure above - Make Evidence section collapsible using HTML details tags - Include relevant data from all tool responses (CloudWatch, DynamoDB, Step Functions, X-Ray) - For CloudWatch: Show complete log group and log stream names without truncation - Present evidence data in code blocks with appropriate source labels ANALYSIS GUIDELINES: - Use multiple tools for comprehensive analysis when needed - Start with document-specific tools for targeted queries - Use system-wide tools for pattern analysis - Combine DynamoDB status with CloudWatch logs for complete picture - Leverage X-Ray for distributed system issues ROOT CAUSE DETERMINATION: 1. Document Status: Check dynamodb_status first 2. Execution Details: Use stepfunction_details for workflow failures 3. Log Analysis: Use cloudwatch_document_logs or cloudwatch_logs for error details 4. Distributed Tracing: Use xray_performance_analysis for service interaction issues 5. Context: Use lambda_lookup for execution environment RECOMMENDATION GUIDELINES: For code-related issues or system bugs: - Do not suggest code modifications - Include error details, timestamps, and context For configuration-related issues: - Direct users to UI configuration panel - Specify exact configuration section and parameter names For operational issues: - Provide immediate troubleshooting steps - Include preventive measures TIME RANGE PARSING: - recent: 1 hour - last week: 168 hours - last day: 24 hours - No time specified: 24 hours (default) IMPORTANT: Do not include any search quality reflections, search quality scores, or meta-analysis sections in your response. Only provide the three required sections: Root Cause, Recommendations, and Evidence.

classes.description: Locator-specific class for "Closing Disclosure" (borrower Closing Disclosure main form only). Match when the document most closely matches the standard borrower Closing Disclosure pages 1-5. First-page cues often include: "Closing Disclosure"; "This form is a statement of final loan terms and closing costs"; "Closing Information"; "Transaction Information"; "Loan Information"; "Loan Terms"; "Projected Payments"; and "Costs at Closing". Continuation pages typically include "Closing Cost Details", "Calculating Cash to Close", "Summaries of Transactions", "Additional Information About This Loan", "Loan Calculations", "Contact Information", and "Confirm Receipt". Do NOT use this class for Addendum to Closing Disclosure pages or Seller Closing Disclosure pages.

Extract this value from the Closing Disclosure. Return the document's stated type/code exactly as shown. On the Closing Disclosure this is typically the delivery method for the integrated disclosure document, such as electronic delivery or another stated method.

SalePriceAlternateAmountText_0532278.description: Alternate sale price/appraised value amount shown in the Closing Information section on Page 1. For purchase loans, this is the 'Sale Price'. Return a plain numeric string with no dollar signs, no commas, optional decimal point, and a leading minus sign for non-zero amounts. Do NOT return the absolute value. Do NOT include '$' or thousand separators.

LicenseStateCode_0532298.description: Lender entity's License State Code — extract ONLY the 2-letter state abbreviation that appears as a prefix in the row label 'ST License ID' on Page 5 Contact Information table. EXAMPLES: row label is 'VA License ID' → return 'VA'; row label is 'TX License ID' → return 'TX'; row label is 'CA License ID' → return 'CA'. Do NOT return the full row label text (e.g., do NOT return 'VA License ID' or 'License ID'). Do NOT return the license number itself (that is field 0532193). Return ONLY the 2-letter state code. Return null if the row label has no state prefix or is not present in the document.

LOAN_CAP_PAYOFFS_AND_PAYMENTS_DETAIL.description: Payoffs and Payments Detail on Page 3 of the Closing Disclosure (left side, above Calculating Cash to Close). Create one object for each populated payoff/payment row showing payee, description, and amount. If the entire section has NO populated rows (all rows blank), still return an array containing exactly ONE placeholder object with all fields set to null (matches AIQ XML behavior where empty sections produce a placeholder dataPoint).
LOAN_CAP_PAYOFFS_AND_PAYMENTS_DETAIL.x-aws-idp-list-item-description: One occurrence/row for Payoffs And Payments.

PayoffPrimaryText_0532455.description: Payoffs And Payments Detail: primary text shown in the TO column for this payoff row, such as the creditor, servicer, or payee name. Return the text exactly as shown.

PayoffPrimaryText_0532456.description: Payoffs And Payments Detail: alternative primary text shown in the TO column for this payoff row when the row includes a creditor name together with account-identifying text. Return the text exactly as shown.

PayoffNarrativeText_0532457.description: Payoffs And Payments Detail: narrative text that follows the payee in the TO column for this row, such as 'to pay off existing loan'. Return the text exactly as shown.

PayoffNarrativeText_0532134.description: Payoffs And Payments Detail: additional narrative text shown in the TO column for this row, such as the liability or payoff description. Return the text exactly as shown.

PayoffAmount_0532135.description: Payoffs And Payments Detail: amount shown in the AMOUNT column for this payoff row. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

DOCUMENT_SPECIFIC_DATA_SET_PAYOFFS_AND_P.description: Payoffs And Payments Total Detail. Single occurrence section from the K. TOTAL PAYOFFS AND PAYMENTS row of the Closing Disclosure.

TotalPayoffsAndPaymentsAmount_0532258.description: Payoffs And Payments Total Detail: total amount shown in the K. TOTAL PAYOFFS AND PAYMENTS row. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

CALCULATING_CASH_TO_CLOSE_COMPARISON_DET.description: Calculating Cash to Close section on Page 3 of the Closing Disclosure. Captures the Cash to Close summary row (singletons) and the variable comparison rows in an array. Row composition differs by loan purpose: purchase loans show items like 'Total Closing Costs (J)', 'Closing Costs Paid Before Closing', 'Closing Costs Financed', 'Down Payment/Funds from Borrower', 'Deposit', 'Funds for Borrower', 'Seller Credits', 'Adjustments and Other Credits'; refinance loans show 'Loan Amount', 'Total Closing Costs (J)', 'Closing Costs Paid Before Closing', 'Total Payoffs and Payments (K)' and have From/To Borrower checkboxes on the Cash to Close row.

CashToCloseLoanEstimateAmount_0532037.description: Cash to Close summary row — Loan Estimate column amount (e.g., '236706', '6704'). Always present. Return a plain numeric string without dollar signs or commas.

CashToCloseFinalAmount_0532038.description: Cash to Close summary row — Final column amount (e.g., '176501.85', '16424.44'). Always present. Return a plain numeric string without dollar signs or commas.

CashToCloseLoanEstimateFromToBorrowerInd.description: Cash to Close summary row — From/To Borrower checkbox in the Loan Estimate column (refinance loans only). Return 'From Borrower' or 'To Borrower' based on which checkbox is checked, or null for purchase loans where this row is just a plain amount without checkboxes.

CashToCloseFinalFromToBorrowerIndicator_.description: Cash to Close summary row — From/To Borrower checkbox in the Final column (refinance loans only). Return 'From Borrower' or 'To Borrower' based on which checkbox is checked, or null for purchase loans where this row is just a plain amount without checkboxes.

ClosingCostsFinancedAmount_0532152.description: Closing Costs Financed (Paid from your Loan Amount) inline amount shown next to the Cash to Close row on refinance loans only (e.g., '$554.47'). Return a plain numeric string without dollar signs or commas, or null when not present (typical for purchase loans).

ComparisonItems.description: Array of comparison rows in the Calculating Cash to Close table. Each item represents one row showing Loan Estimate vs Final amounts and whether they changed. Number of rows varies by loan purpose: purchase loans typically have 8 rows, refinance loans typically have 4 rows. Skip the bottom 'Cash to Close' summary row — that's captured by the singleton fields above.

CalculatingCashToCloseItem_0532016.description: Calculating Cash to Close row label. Official Appendix C section 10 uses row-specific IDs 10.1 through 10.8; this AWS schema uses one repeated row object with 0532016 as the row label. Extract the left-column label exactly, excluding Loan Estimate/Final/Did this change values.

LoanEstimateAmount_0532013.description: Calculating Cash to Close Loan Estimate amount for the same row as CalculatingCashToCloseItem_0532016. Corresponds to the 10.x.1 Loan Estimate amount lane. Return numeric string; preserve negative sign when shown with a minus.

FinalAmount_0532014.description: Calculating Cash to Close Final amount for the same row as CalculatingCashToCloseItem_0532016. Corresponds to the 10.x.2 Final amount lane. Return numeric string; preserve negative sign when shown with a minus.

DidChangeIndicator_0532015.description: Calculating Cash to Close 'Did this change?' indicator for the same row as CalculatingCashToCloseItem_0532016. Corresponds to 10.x.3. Return YES or NO when shown; return null if the row has no indicator.

DidChangeExplanationText_0532247.description: Calculating Cash to Close explanatory text for the same row as CalculatingCashToCloseItem_0532016. Corresponds to 10.x.4. Extract only the explanation after the YES/NO indicator; return null when no explanation is present.

SellerCreditsLoanEstimateAmount_0532810.description: Special Seller Credits Loan Estimate amount. Use this field ONLY when CalculatingCashToCloseItem is 'Seller Credits'; otherwise return null. Return a plain numeric string without dollar signs or commas.

AdjustmentsAndOtherCreditsLoanEstimateAm.description: Special Adjustments and Other Credits Loan Estimate amount. Use this field ONLY when CalculatingCashToCloseItem is 'Adjustments and Other Credits'; otherwise return null. Return a plain numeric string without dollar signs or commas.

BORROWER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_K_DET.description: Borrower's Transaction K Detail. Single occurrence section from the Borrower's Transaction summary on page 3 of the Closing Disclosure. Contains the K. Due from Borrower at Closing total amount.

TotalDueFromBorrowerAtClosingAmount_0532.description: Borrower's Transaction K Detail: total amount shown in the K. Due from Borrower at Closing heading row. Return a plain numeric string only, without dollar signs or commas (e.g. '189762.30'). Return null if not present.

LOAN_CAP_INTEGRATED_DISCLOSURE_BORROWER_.description: Borrower's Transaction K Line Items Detail. Create one object for each populated row in the K.01-K.04 area of the Borrower's Transaction summary on page 3. Common rows include: Sale Price of Property (K.01), Sale Price of Any Personal Property Included in Sale (K.02), Closing Costs Paid at Closing (J) (K.03), and any additional items (K.04). Skip blank rows.

BorrowerTransactionKItemType_0532043.description: Borrower's Transaction K line-item label. Official Appendix C shows K.01 Sale Price of Property, K.02 Sale Price of Any Personal Property Included in Sale, K.03 Closing Costs Paid at Closing (J), K.04 Liability Item, and K.05 adjustment lines. Extract the visible label for the row, not the amount.

BorrowerTransactionKItemAmount_0532044.description: Borrower's Transaction K line-item amount corresponding to BorrowerTransactionKItemType_0532043. Return numeric string without dollar sign/commas. Keep in the same row object as the label.

BORROWER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_K_ITE.description: Borrower’s Transaction K Items Paid by Seller in Advance. Create one object for each visible line in the Adjustments for Items Paid by Seller in Advance subsection of Borrower’s Transaction K.
BORROWER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_K_ITE.x-aws-idp-list-item-description: One occurrence/row for Borrower’s Transaction K items paid by seller in advance.

ItemLabel_0532057.description: Borrower’s Transaction K Items Paid by Seller in Advance: item label text shown for this row. Return the text exactly as shown.

ItemFromDate_0532058.description: Borrower’s Transaction K Items Paid by Seller in Advance: from date shown for this row. Return as YYYY-MM-DD when clear, otherwise return the source date text exactly as shown.

ItemToDate_0532059.description: Borrower’s Transaction K Items Paid by Seller in Advance: to date shown for this row. Return as YYYY-MM-DD when clear, otherwise return the source date text exactly as shown.

ItemAmount_0532060.description: Borrower’s Transaction K Items Paid by Seller in Advance: amount shown for this row. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

BORROWER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_L_DET.description: Borrower's Transaction L Detail. Single occurrence section from the L. Paid Already by or on Behalf of Borrower at Closing summary on page 3 of the Closing Disclosure. Contains the L total amount and an array of L.01-L.05 line items (Deposit, Loan Amount, Existing Loans Assumed, additional items, Seller Credit).

TotalPaidByOrOnBehalfOfBorrowerAtClosing.description: Borrower's Transaction L Detail: total amount shown in the L. Paid Already by or on Behalf of Borrower at Closing heading row. Return a plain numeric string only, without dollar signs or commas (e.g. '175615.04'). Return null if not present.

BorrowerTransactionLLineItems.description: Array of L.01-L.05 line items in the L. Paid Already by or on Behalf of Borrower at Closing section. Each item has a Label (PaidAlreadyItemType_0532294) and EITHER a regular Amount (PaidAlreadyItemAmount_0532062) for L.01-L.04 rows OR a Seller Credit Amount (CapSellerCreditAmount_0532751) when the label is 'Seller Credit' (L.05). Common labels include: 'Deposit' (L.01), 'Loan Amount' (L.02), 'Existing Loan(s) Assumed or Taken Subject to' (L.03), additional items (L.04), and 'Seller Credit' (L.05). Skip blank rows. Use 0532751 as the amount field ONLY when the Label is exactly 'Seller Credit'; for all other rows use 0532062.

PaidAlreadyItemType_0532294.description: Borrower's Transaction L line-item label. Official Appendix C Section 12 includes Deposit, Loan Amount, Existing Loan(s) Assumed or Taken Subject to, Proceeds of Subordinate Lien(s), and Seller Credit. Extract the visible label for the row, not the amount.

PaidAlreadyItemAmount_0532062.description: Borrower's Transaction L regular line-item amount for the same row as PaidAlreadyItemType_0532294. Use for non-Seller-Credit rows. Return null when the row label is Seller Credit, because Seller Credit uses 0532751.

CapSellerCreditAmount_0532751.description: Borrower's Transaction L Seller Credit amount, official Appendix C field 12.5.1. Use only when the same row label is Seller Credit. Return null for all other L rows.

BORROWER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_L_OTH.description: Borrower’s Transaction L Other Credits. Create one object for each visible line in the Other Credits subsection of Borrower’s Transaction L.
BORROWER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_L_OTH.x-aws-idp-list-item-description: One occurrence/row for Borrower’s Transaction L other credits.

OtherCreditLabel_0532068.description: Borrower’s Transaction L Other Credits: label text shown for this row. Return the text exactly as shown.

OtherCreditSecondaryText_0532296.description: Borrower’s Transaction L Other Credits: additional payee, source, or descriptive text shown for this row. Return the text exactly as shown.

OtherCreditAmount_0532069.description: Borrower’s Transaction L Other Credits: amount shown for this row. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

BORROWER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_L_ADJ.description: Borrower’s Transaction L Adjustment Lines. Create one object for each visible adjustment line in Borrower’s Transaction L.

BORROWER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_L_ITE.x-aws-idp-list-item-description: One occurrence/row for Borrower’s Transaction L items unpaid by seller.

ItemLabel_0532081.description: Borrower’s Transaction L Items Unpaid by Seller: item label text shown for this row. Return the text exactly as shown.

ItemToDate_0532083.description: Borrower’s Transaction L Items Unpaid by Seller: to date shown for this row. Return as YYYY-MM-DD when clear, otherwise return the source date text exactly as shown.

ItemAmount_0532084.description: Borrower’s Transaction L Items Unpaid by Seller: amount shown for this row. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

BORROWER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_CALCU.description: Borrower’s Transaction Calculation Detail. Single occurrence calculation section at the bottom of the Borrower’s Transaction summary on page 3 of the Closing Disclosure.

TotalDueFromBorrowerAtClosingAmount_0532.description: Borrower’s Transaction Calculation Detail: Total Due from Borrower at Closing amount. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

TotalPaidByOrOnBehalfOfBorrowerAtClosing.description: Borrower’s Transaction Calculation Detail: Total Paid Already by or on Behalf of Borrower at Closing amount. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

CashToCloseDirectionIndicator_0532087.description: Borrower’s Transaction Calculation Detail: checkbox or indicator showing whether Cash to Close is From Borrower or To Borrower. Return the displayed direction text exactly as shown.

CashToCloseAmount_0532244.description: Borrower’s Transaction Calculation Detail: Cash to Close amount shown on the final calculation line. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

SELLER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_M_DETAI.description: Seller’s Transaction M Detail. Single occurrence section from the Seller’s Transaction summary on page 3 of the Closing Disclosure.

TotalDueToSellerAtClosingAmount_0532088.description: Seller’s Transaction M Detail: total amount shown in the M. Due to Seller at Closing heading row. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

PrimaryLineLabel_0532091.description: Seller’s Transaction M Detail: primary line label text shown within the section when present. Return the text exactly as shown.

PrimaryLineAmount_0532092.description: Seller’s Transaction M Detail: amount shown for the primary visible line item. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

SELLER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_M_ITEMS.description: Seller’s Transaction M Items Paid by Seller in Advance. Create one object for each visible line in the Adjustments for Items Paid by Seller in Advance subsection of Seller’s Transaction M.
SELLER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_M_ITEMS.x-aws-idp-list-item-description: One occurrence/row for Seller’s Transaction M items paid by seller in advance.

ItemLabel_0532102.description: Seller’s Transaction M Items Paid by Seller in Advance: item label text shown for this row. Return the text exactly as shown.

ItemFromDate_0532103.description: Seller’s Transaction M Items Paid by Seller in Advance: from date shown for this row. Return as YYYY-MM-DD when clear, otherwise return the source date text exactly as shown.

ItemAmount_0532105.description: Seller’s Transaction M Items Paid by Seller in Advance: amount shown for this row. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

SELLER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_N_DETAI.description: Seller’s Transaction N Detail. Single occurrence section from the N. Due from Seller at Closing portion of the Seller’s Transaction summary on page 3 of the Closing Disclosure.

TotalDueFromSellerAtClosingAmount_053210.description: Seller’s Transaction N Detail: total amount shown in the N. Due from Seller at Closing heading row. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

PrimaryLineLabel_0532112.description: Seller’s Transaction N Detail: primary line label text shown within the section when present. Return the text exactly as shown.

PrimaryLineAmount_0532113.description: Seller’s Transaction N Detail: amount shown for the primary visible line item. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

SELLER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_N_ITEMS.description: Seller’s Transaction N Items Unpaid by Seller. Create one object for each visible line in the Adjustments for Items Unpaid by Seller subsection of Seller’s Transaction N.
SELLER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_N_ITEMS.x-aws-idp-list-item-description: One occurrence/row for Seller’s Transaction N items unpaid by seller.

ItemLabel_0532126.description: Seller’s Transaction N Items Unpaid by Seller: item label text shown for this row. Return the text exactly as shown.

ItemToDate_0532128.description: Seller’s Transaction N Items Unpaid by Seller: to date shown for this row. Return as YYYY-MM-DD when clear, otherwise return the source date text exactly as shown.

ItemAmount_0532129.description: Seller’s Transaction N Items Unpaid by Seller: amount shown for this row. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

SELLER_SUMMARIES_OF_TRANSACTIONS_CALCULA.description: Seller’s Transaction Calculation Detail. Single occurrence calculation section at the bottom of the Seller’s Transaction summary on page 3 of the Closing Disclosure.

TotalDueToSellerAtClosingAmount_0532130.description: Seller’s Transaction Calculation Detail: Total Due to Seller at Closing amount. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

TotalDueFromSellerAtClosingAmount_053213.description: Seller’s Transaction Calculation Detail: Total Due from Seller at Closing amount. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

CashToSellerDirectionIndicator_0532133.description: Seller’s Transaction Calculation Detail: checkbox or indicator showing whether the final cash line is Cash From Seller or Cash To Seller. Return the displayed direction text exactly as shown.

CashToSellerAmount_0532256.description: Seller’s Transaction Calculation Detail: final Cash To/From Seller amount shown on the last calculation line. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no currency symbols, commas, or surrounding text.

LOAN_CAP_LATE_CHARGE_RULE_DETAIL.description: Loan Disclosures - Late Payment Detail. Single occurrence section from the Closing Disclosure.

LateChargeGracePeriodDaysCount_0532157.description: Loan Disclosures - Late Payment Detail: number of days late before the lender will charge a late fee. Return the numeric value only, without extra words.

LateChargeRatePercent_0532158.description: Loan Disclosures - Late Payment Detail: late fee rate percent. Return a plain numeric string only, with optional decimal point, and no percent sign or surrounding text. Return the numeric value only; strip any '%' symbol from the document. Example: '4%' -> '4'; '6.875%' -> '6.875'.

LateChargeAssessmentBasisText_0532442.description: Loan Disclosures - Late Payment Detail: text describing the payment basis used for the late fee, such as monthly principal and interest. Return the text exactly as shown.

LOAN_CAP_NEGATIVE_AMORTIZATION_DETAIL.description: Loan Disclosures - Negative Amortization Detail. Single occurrence section from page 4 of the Closing Disclosure that captures the borrower's negative amortization feature status. The form has 3 checkbox options: two affirmative options ('are scheduled to make' / 'may have monthly payments') captured by 0532159, and one negative option ('do not have') captured by 0532160.

NegativeAmortizationType_0532159.description: Loan Disclosures - Negative Amortization affirmative option indicator. Captures which of the TWO affirmative checkboxes is selected on the form. Return one of: 'are scheduled to make' (when first checkbox is checked - guaranteed negative amortization), 'may have' (when second checkbox is checked - possible negative amortization), or null if neither affirmative checkbox is checked (i.e., the borrower selected 'do not have'). Do NOT return 'Checked'/'Unchecked' here — return the actual option text or null.

NegativeAmortizationAbsentIndicator_0532.description: Loan Disclosures - Negative Amortization 'do not have' checkbox indicator. Return 'Checked' if the 'do not have a negative amortization feature' checkbox is checked, 'Unchecked' otherwise. This is the most common case for typical fixed-rate and standard ARM loans.

LOAN_CAP_PARTIAL_PAYMENT_DETAIL.description: Loan Disclosures - Partial Payments Detail. Single occurrence section from the Closing Disclosure.

PartialPaymentsAcceptedIndicator_0532161.description: Loan Disclosures - Partial Payments Detail: indicator showing the option that the lender may accept partial payments. Return 'Checked' or 'Unchecked' exactly.

PartialPaymentsNotAcceptedIndicator_0532.description: Loan Disclosures - Partial Payments Detail: indicator showing the option that the lender does not accept partial payments. Return 'Checked' or 'Unchecked' exactly.

SECURITY_INTEREST_PROPERTY_ADDRESS.description: Security Interest Property Address from the Loan Disclosures section on Page 4 of the Closing Disclosure. Captures the property address where the borrower is granting a security interest, broken into 4 components (street, city, state, ZIP).

SecurityInterestStreetAddressText_053216.description: Street address line of the Security Interest property address on Page 4 (e.g., '456 Somewhere Ave'). Return the street portion only without city/state/ZIP.

SecurityInterestCityText_0532165.description: City portion of the Security Interest property address on Page 4 (e.g., 'Anytown'). Return only the city name.

SecurityInterestStateCodeText_0532166.description: State code portion of the Security Interest property address on Page 4 (e.g., 'ST', 'CA', 'NJ'). Return the 2-letter state code.

SecurityInterestPostalCodeText_0532167.description: ZIP/postal code portion of the Security Interest property address on Page 4 (e.g., '12345', '07748-3303'). Return only the ZIP code.

ESCROW_ACCOUNT_DETAIL.description: Escrow Account Detail. Single occurrence section from page 4 of the Closing Disclosure.

EscrowAccountForNowIndicator_0532169.description: Escrow Account Detail: indicator showing whether the loan will have an escrow account for now. Return 'Checked' or 'Unchecked' exactly.

Captures the four payment-pattern types (Interest Only, Optional, Step, Seasonal) with their indicators, payment counts, and payment-sequence ranges. A single loan has 0 or 1 AP Table. Seasonal Payments uniquely has 2 from/to scenarios.

InterestOnlyPaymentsIndicator_0532300.description: Adjustable Payment (AP) Table Detail: indicator showing whether the loan has interest-only payments. Return 'YES' or 'NO' exactly.

OptionalPaymentsIndicator_0532302.description: Adjustable Payment (AP) Table Detail: indicator showing whether the loan has optional payments. Return 'YES' or 'NO' exactly.

FirstChangeAmount_0532310.description: Adjustable Payment (AP) Table Detail: First Change / Amount value. Return the text exactly as shown.

OptionalPaymentsToSequenceText_0532448.description: Optional Payments — to-payment sequence number. Same pattern as Interest Only. Return as a numeric string. Return null when not applicable.

StepPaymentsToSequenceText_0532450.description: Step Payments — to-payment sequence number. Same pattern as Interest Only. Return as a numeric string. Return null when not applicable.

SeasonalPaymentsScenario1FromSequenceTex.description: Seasonal Payments Scenario 1 — from-payment sequence number. The first scenario for Seasonal Payments. Return as a numeric string. Return null when not applicable.

SeasonalPaymentsScenario1ToSequenceText_.description: Seasonal Payments Scenario 1 — to-payment sequence number. Return as a numeric string. Return null when not applicable.

SeasonalPaymentsScenario2FromSequenceTex.description: Seasonal Payments Scenario 2 — from-payment sequence number. The second scenario for Seasonal Payments (some loans show two seasonal scenarios). Return as a numeric string. Return null when only one Seasonal scenario applies.

SeasonalPaymentsScenario2ToSequenceText_.description: Seasonal Payments Scenario 2 — to-payment sequence number. Return as a numeric string. Return null when only one Seasonal scenario applies.

BorrowerAddressLineText_0532279.description: First line of the Borrower address shown in the Transaction Information section on Page 1, under 'Borrower'. This is the street address (e.g., '123 Anywhere Street'). The green box 2279 marks the START of the borrower address span.

BorrowerAddressCityStateZipText_0532282.description: Second line of the Borrower address (city, state, ZIP). e.g., 'Anytown, ST 12345'. The green box 2282 marks the END of the borrower address span.

SELLER_ADDRESS.description: Seller Address from the Transaction Information section on Page 1 of the Closing Disclosure. Captures the seller's address (purchase loans only). Return null fields for refinance loans where there is no seller.

SellerAddressLineText_0532230.description: First line of the Seller address shown in the Transaction Information section on Page 1, under 'Seller'. The green box 2230 marks the START of the seller address span.

SellerAddressCityStateZipText_0532233.description: Second line of the Seller address (city, state, ZIP). The green box 2233 marks the END of the seller address span.

SellerAddressPostalText_0532820.description: Postal/ZIP code portion of the Seller address. The green box 2820 covers the postal span specifically.

LENDER_DETAIL.description: Lender Detail from the Transaction Information section on Page 1 of the Closing Disclosure. Captures the Lender name as shown on the form.

LenderNameText_0532237.description: Lender name shown in the Transaction Information section on Page 1, on the 'Lender' row (e.g., 'Ficus Bank').

COSTS_AT_CLOSING_DETAIL.description: Costs at Closing section from Page 1 of the Closing Disclosure. Captures the total Closing Costs amount and its narrative breakdown (Loan Costs, Other Costs, Lender Credits).

ClosingCostsAmountText_0531884.description: Total Closing Costs amount shown in the Costs at Closing section on Page 1 (e.g., '$14,530.82'). Return a plain numeric string without dollar signs or commas (e.g., '14530.82').

ClosingCostsDescriptionNarrativeText_053.description: Closing Costs description narrative shown next to the total amount on Page 1. Example: 'Includes $6,775.00 in Loan Costs + $7,755.82 in Other Costs - $0 in Lender Credits'. Return the full narrative text as displayed.

LoanCostsIncludedAmountText_0532241.description: Loan Costs amount included in the Closing Costs description narrative on Page 1 (e.g., $6,775.00 from 'Includes $6,775.00 in Loan Costs'). Return a plain numeric string without dollar signs or commas.

OtherCostsIncludedAmountText_0532242.description: Other Costs amount included in the Closing Costs description narrative on Page 1 (e.g., $7,755.82). Return a plain numeric string without dollar signs or commas.

LenderCreditsIncludedAmountText_0532243.description: Lender Credits amount included in the Closing Costs description narrative on Page 1 of the Closing Disclosure. Located in the Costs at Closing table, in the 'Closing Costs' row narrative phrase: 'Includes $X in Loan Costs + $Y in Other Costs - $Z in Lender Credits'. IMPORTANT — SIGN CONVENTION: The Lender Credits amount must be returned as a NEGATIVE number or ZERO (must be <= 0). The form displays a leading minus/dash sign before the dollar amount (e.g., '– $500'), indicating it reduces total closing costs. EXAMPLES: form shows '– $500.00' → return '-500.00'; form shows '– $1,138.80' → return '-1138.80'; form shows '$0' or empty → return '0' or '0.00'. Return a plain numeric string with no dollar signs, no commas, optional decimal point, and a leading minus sign for non-zero amounts. Do NOT return the absolute value (e.g., '500.00' is WRONG when there is a minus on the form). Do NOT include '$' or thousand separators.

PROJECTED_PAYMENT_LABELS_DETAIL.description: Projected Payment row label frequency descriptors. Captures the period/frequency word (e.g., 'Monthly', 'Weekly', 'Yearly') shown in the row labels of the Projected Payments table on Page 1.

PrincipalAndInterestPaymentFrequencyType.description: Period/frequency descriptor from the 'Principal & Interest' row label in the Projected Payments table on Page 1. Extract the frequency word from the label (e.g., 'Monthly Principal & Interest' returns 'Monthly'). Possible values: 'Monthly', 'Weekly', 'Yearly', or other frequency word as shown.

EstimatedTotalPaymentFrequencyType_05322.description: Period/frequency descriptor from the 'Estimated Total Monthly Payment' row label in the Projected Payments table on Page 1. Extract the frequency word from the label (e.g., 'Estimated Total Monthly Payment' returns 'Monthly'). Possible values: 'Monthly', 'Weekly', 'Yearly', or other frequency word as shown.

LOAN_INTEREST_RATE_ADJUSTMENT.description: Interest Rate adjustment narrative descriptors from the Loan Terms section on Page 1 of the Closing Disclosure. Captures the individual elements of phrases like 'Adjusts every 3 years starting in year 6' and 'Can go as high as 12% in year 14' shown on the Interest Rate row when the rate is adjustable.

InterestRateAdjustmentFrequencyAdverbTex.description: Frequency adverb word in the Interest Rate adjustment phrase on Page 1 (e.g., 'every' from 'Adjusts every 3 years'). Return null when the loan has a fixed interest rate.

InterestRateAdjustmentFrequencyCountText.description: Frequency count in the Interest Rate adjustment phrase on Page 1 (e.g., '3' from 'Adjusts every 3 years'). Return as a numeric string. Return null for fixed rate loans.

InterestRateAdjustmentFrequencyUnitText_.description: Frequency unit in the Interest Rate adjustment phrase on Page 1 (e.g., 'years' from 'Adjusts every 3 years'). Possible values: 'year(s)', 'month(s)', 'week(s)', 'day(s)'. Return null for fixed rate loans.

InterestRateAdjustmentFirstChangeYearTex.description: First-change year in the Interest Rate adjustment phrase on Page 1 (e.g., '6' from 'starting in year 6'). Return as a numeric string. Return null for fixed rate loans.

InterestRateMaximumPercentText_0532389.description: Maximum interest rate percentage shown in the 'Can go as high as X%' phrase on Page 1 (e.g., '12'). Strip any '%' symbol from the displayed value — return only the numeric percent value as a plain string. Return null for fixed rate loans. Return the numeric value only; strip any '%' symbol from the document. Example: '4%' -> '4'; '6.875%' -> '6.875'.

InterestRateMaximumYearUnitText_0532390.description: Period unit in the 'Can go as high as 12% in year X' phrase on Page 1 (e.g., 'year'). Return null for fixed rate loans.

InterestRateMaximumYearText_0532391.description: Year value in the 'Can go as high as 12% in year X' phrase on Page 1 (e.g., '14'). Return as a numeric string. Return null for fixed rate loans.

InterestRateAIRTableReferenceText_053239.description: Reference text pointing to the AIR Table from the Loan Terms Interest Rate row on Page 1. Example: 'See AIR Table on page 2 for details'. Return the full reference text. Return null for fixed rate loans.

LoanTermIncreaseMaximumCountText_0532459.description: Loan Term increase maximum count value, shown in the Loan Terms section on Page 1 in 'Can increase loan term to X years' (e.g., '35'). Return only the numeric value. Pair with LoanTermIncreaseMaximumUnitText_0532460 for the unit. Return null when the loan term cannot increase.

LoanTermIncreaseMaximumUnitText_0532460.description: Loan Term increase maximum period unit, shown in the Loan Terms section on Page 1 in 'Can increase loan term to 35 X' (e.g., 'years'). Return the unit word as shown. Return null when the loan term cannot increase.

LOAN_PAYMENT_ADJUSTMENT.description: Monthly Principal & Interest payment adjustment narrative descriptors from the Loan Terms section on Page 1 of the Closing Disclosure. PaymentAdjustmentFrequencyCountText_0532.description: Frequency count in the P&I payment adjustment phrase on Page 1 (e.g., '3' from 'Adjusts every 3 years'). Return as a numeric string. Return null for fixed payment loans.

classes.description: Classification-only class for "Addendum to Closing Disclosure". Match when the document most closely matches a borrower Closing Disclosure addendum or overflow page. First-page cues often include "Addendum to Closing Disclosure" or "ADDENDUM TO BORROWER CLOSING DISCLOSURE". These pages commonly continue the borrower Closing Disclosure with a "Payoffs and Payments" table or other overflow content. Do NOT use this class for the main borrower Closing Disclosure pages 1-5 or for Seller Closing Disclosure. No extraction schema is configured for this class.

Classification-only class for "Seller Closing Disclosure". Match when the document most closely matches seller-side Closing Disclosure pages, including headers such as "Seller Closing Disclosure" or "Estimated Closing Disclosure". First-page cues often include seller-focused "Summaries of Transactions", "Seller's Transaction", "Cash From/To Seller", seller-paid columns, seller contact information, and confirm receipt. Do NOT use this class for borrower Closing Disclosure pages or Addendum to Closing Disclosure pages. No extraction schema is configured for this class.
Catch-all class for pages/documents that are not clearly Borrower Closing Disclosure, Addendum to Closing Disclosure, or Seller Closing Disclosure.

auto_split.system_prompt: You are an expert document analyst. Your task is to identify distinct document sections within a multi-page document package. Return only valid JSON.

rules.system_prompt: You are an expert in analyzing policy documents and extracting business rules, regulations, and compliance requirements. Extract rules as structured, actionable validation statements.
rules.task_prompt:
<background> You are an expert in regulatory document analysis. Your task is to extract ALL business rules, regulations, compliance requirements, and validation criteria from the attached policy document as a single flat list of rules. </background>
<task> Analyze the policy document thoroughly, page by page. For each rule found:
1. Give the rule a short, descriptive snake_case name (e.g., "bundled_services_not_separate",
   "modifier_59_distinct_sites", "prior_auth_required")

2. Write a clear, actionable description — phrased as a yes/no validation question
   or checkable statement

3. Include specific codes, numbers, thresholds, or conditions mentioned
4. Note any exceptions or special cases within the description
5. Reference the page number or section where the rule was found </task>
<extraction-guidelines> Guidelines: - Extract rules VERBATIM from the document where possible - Each rule should be a self-contained, evaluable statement - Rules should be specific enough to validate a transaction against - Do NOT classify or categorize the rules — output one flat list - Use descriptive snake_case names as rule identifiers (NOT generic "rule_1", "rule_2") - Each rule description should be phrased as a validation question or checkable statement - Do NOT interpret or infer rules not explicitly stated in the document - Process ALL pages of the document - If a rule has exceptions, include them in the description </extraction-guidelines>
<output-format> Return ONLY a valid JSON array containing exactly ONE object. All rules go into a single "rule_properties" dict with descriptive snake_case keys:
[
    {
        "x-aws-idp-rule-type": "policy_rules",
        "description": "All rules extracted from the policy document",
        "rule_properties": {
            "bundled_services_not_separate": {
                "description": "Are bundled services (exploration, lysis of adhesions, closure, anesthesia) NOT reported separately from the primary surgical procedure?",
                "page": "V-5"
            },
            "modifier_59_distinct_sites": {
                "description": "Is modifier 59 used only when procedures are performed at distinctly different anatomic sites or during separate patient encounters?",
                "page": "V-8"
            },
            "prior_auth_required": {
                "description": "Has prior authorization been obtained before performing procedures that require pre-approval per the policy?",
                "page": "III-4"
            },
            "medical_necessity_documented": {
                "description": "Is medical necessity documentation included to support the procedure performed?",
                "page": "IV-1"
            }
        }
    }
] </output-format>
<final-instructions> Extract rules from the attached policy document by: 1. Reading every page systematically to identify all stated rules 2. Giving each rule a short, descriptive snake_case name 3. Phrasing each rule as a yes/no validation question 4. Including page references for traceability 5. Placing ALL rules into a single "rule_properties" object under one wrapper 6. Do NOT include any text outside the JSON array - return ONLY valid JSON </final-instructions>

llm_method.task_prompt:
I need to evaluate attribute extraction for a document of class: {DOCUMENT_CLASS}.

For the attribute named "{ATTRIBUTE_NAME}" described as "{ATTRIBUTE_DESCRIPTION}":
- Expected value: {EXPECTED_VALUE}
- Actual value: {ACTUAL_VALUE}

Do these values match in meaning, taking into account formatting differences, word order, abbreviations, and semantic equivalence?
Provide your assessment as a JSON with three fields:
- "match": boolean (true if they match, false if not)
- "score": number between 0 and 1 representing the confidence/similarity score
- "reason": brief explanation of your decision

Respond ONLY with the JSON and nothing else. Here's the exact format:
{
  "match": true or false,
  "score": 0.0 to 1.0,
  "reason": "Your explanation here"
}
llm_method.system_prompt: You are an evaluator that helps determine if the predicted and expected values match for document attribute extraction. You will consider the context and meaning rather than just exact string matching.
